commit.message:
fix: write barrier/bulk acks directly under writeMu, not via barrierCh

The barrierCh priority channel doesn't help when sendLoop is blocked
on writeMu (held by BulkSync per-session writes). The ack sits in
barrierCh while sendLoop can't read it. Write acks directly under
writeMu instead — they'll be sent as soon as the current BulkSync
per-session write releases the lock (<1ms).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Ajoute workflow GitHub Actions pour build Jekyll

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: resolve SSE reconnection loop with separate /sse + /messages endpoints (v2.46.1) (#699)

Fix SSE clients entering rapid reconnection loops because POST /mcp
never routed messages to SSEServerTransport.handlePostMessage() (#617).

Root cause: SSE sessions were stored in a separate `this.session` property
invisible to the StreamableHTTP POST handler. The POST handler only
checked `this.transports` (StreamableHTTP map), so SSE messages were
never delivered, causing immediate reconnection and rate limiter exhaustion.

Changes:
- Add GET /sse + POST /messages endpoints following the official MCP SDK
  backward-compatible server pattern (separate endpoints per transport)
- Store SSE transports in the shared this.transports map with instanceof
  guards for type discrimination
- Remove legacy this.session singleton, resetSessionSSE(), and isExpired()
- Extract duplicated auth logic into authenticateRequest() method
- Add Bearer token auth and rate limiting to SSE endpoints
- Add skipSuccessfulRequests to authLimiter to prevent 429 storms
- Mark SSE transport as deprecated (removed in MCP SDK v2.x)

The handleRequest() codepath used by the downstream SaaS backend
(N8NMCPEngine.processRequest()) is unchanged. Session persistence
(exportSessionState/restoreSessionState) is unchanged.

Closes #617

Conceived by Romuald Członkowski - https://www.aiadvisors.pl/en

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix(backup): guard resetProgress, wrap coroutines in try-catch, add DataStore comment

- resetProgress() now no-ops if state is Running to prevent clobbering in-flight operations
- Both export and restore serviceScope.launch bodies wrapped in try/catch; CancellationException
  is rethrown, unexpected exceptions report BackupProgress.Error so the service never gets stuck
- Add inline comment in RestoreRepository noting that overwriting .preferences_pb on disk does
  not invalidate in-memory DataStore caches; full process restart (already in BackupScreen) required

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: classifieds discovery — Claude finds search results page URL, Playwright extracts listings from it (v1.0.84)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Optimize FFmpeg transcoding: ultrafast preset + zerolatency

Reduces CPU usage and latency for HEVC-to-H.264 live transcoding.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix upload_single_strand.sh: DELETE before MOVE, longer retry sleep

HTTP 423 Locked occurs when a previous failed upload left a server-side
lock on the destination. Fix:
- DELETE destination before MOVE to clear stale locks
- Retry DELETE on each subsequent attempt too
- Increase sleep between retries 5s → 30s
- Increase max attempts 5 → 8

Co-authored-by: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

Structured profile cards for agent calibration UI (#155)

Agent now outputs sample profiles as [PROFILE]{json}[/PROFILE] tags
instead of markdown text. The frontend parses these into proper
structured cards with:

- Avatar initials + name + index badge (1/3)
- Title @ Company
- Location + years of experience
- Trajectory (Company1 → Company2 → Company3)
- Tech tags (K8s, AWS, Terraform...)
- Strengths (✓ green) and concerns (⚠ yellow)

The cards are visually distinct from the rest of the chat, making
calibration much clearer than a wall of emoji-laden text.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01Bn8fgS1UjnESi1FbCCkouU

Co-authored-by: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

Initial implementation of kindle-email

Self-hosted app that watches a Gmail label (SendToKindle), converts
forwarded newsletters to EPUB via readability-lxml + BeautifulSoup,
and delivers them to a Kindle via Send-to-Kindle email.

Features: IMAP label polling, MIME parsing, content extraction,
SSRF-protected image downloading, EPUB generation, SMTP delivery,
Docker support, 17 passing tests.

commit.message:
feat(auth): Add post_authentication_redirect configuration hook (#172)

Host apps that use panda-core for auth may need custom routing after
OAuth login — for example, redirecting to a workspace picker in
multi-tenant setups. This hook lets them inject a Proc that receives
(user, request) and returns a redirect URL, or nil to fall through
to the existing redirect logic.

The hook runs after session creation and activity logging, but before
the existing origin_subdomain redirect. Uses allow_other_host: true
so cross-subdomain redirects work. Errors in the hook are caught and
logged, falling through to the default redirect rather than leaving
the user in an inconsistent state.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Agent calibration: Juicebox-style 3-profile sample flow (#154)

* Add Market Insights panel in mission sourcing

New feature: before launching a search, users can analyze the market
to understand the talent pool.

Backend (database-search):
- New 'insights' action: samples 100 profiles from Apollo (no enrichment,
  no credits consumed) and aggregates analytics
- Returns: totalAvailable, topLocations, topCompanies, topTitles,
  seniorityDistribution

Frontend:
- MarketInsightsPanel component with bar chart visualizations
- Shows after filters are generated, triggered by 'Analyser le marché' button
- 4 analytics sections: Location, Entreprises, Titres, Séniorité
- Displays total available profiles from Apollo

Inspired by Juicebox Talent Insights — gives recruiters market intelligence
before they commit to a search strategy.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01Bn8fgS1UjnESi1FbCCkouU

* Rewrite agent system prompt with Juicebox-style calibration flow

New 3-phase agent flow:

Phase 1 — DIAGNOSTIC (1 message):
- Quick summary of the role
- Estimated talent pool size
- Recommended strategy
- Immediate transition to calibration

Phase 2 — CALIBRATION BY SAMPLES (key innovation):
- Agent presents 3 realistic sample profiles
- User approves/rejects each with feedback
- Agent adapts criteria based on rejections
- Loop until 2/3+ approved
- Varied profiles: ideal / edge case / different angle

Phase 3 — PLAN & LAUNCH:
- Clear search plan summary
- Options: launch / adjust / new samples

Inspired by Juicebox Agents' 'approve 3 consecutive profiles'
calibration model. The agent learns what the recruiter wants
from concrete examples, not abstract filter questions.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01Bn8fgS1UjnESi1FbCCkouU

---------

Fix unprotected StringWriter pattern across all test files

it to all remaining test files.

Add terminal compatibility section to README and about-dynadocs

and iTerm2 (macOS recommended). Also updates dispatch-and-messaging.md
to remove unverified terminal claims.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix upload_single_strand.sh: arithmetic increment with set -e

(( count++ )) evaluates to 0 (false) when count starts at 0, which
exits the script under set -e. Replace all post-increment expressions
with count=$(( count + 1 )) to avoid the silent exit.
